task_completion.description: Agent creates a Python script to model the solar water heating system using PySAM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing a Python script that imports PySAM (e.g., `import pysam.Swh` or similar) and initializes the Swh module.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the required system parameters in the script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The script contains assignments for the specified parameters, such as 2 (collectors), 2.98 (area), 33.4 (tilt), 180 (azimuth), 303 (tank volume), 55 (set temperature), etc.

task_completion.description: Agent executes the Python script to run the simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the execution of the Python script (e.g., `python swh_model.py`) without throwing fatal errors.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the results to the specified JSON file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal (e.g., `ls`) or file explorer showing the file `swh_results.json` exists in `/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/`.

task_completion.description: The JSON output file contains the required keys and structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of `swh_results.json` are visible (via `cat` or an editor) and contain the keys `annual_energy_kwh`, `solar_fraction`, `monthly_energy_kwh` (with an array of 12 numbers), and `system_parameters` (containing ncoll, area_coll, tilt, azimuth, V_tank).

integrity.description: The agent actually ran a PySAM simulation rather than hardcoding fake results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script contains a call to execute the PySAM model (e.g., `module.execute()`) and dynamically extracts the results to write to the JSON file, rather than hardcoding the output values.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type out the final JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON file is generated programmatically by the Python script (using `json.dump` or similar), not manually typed in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used a valid weather file as instructed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script sets the weather file path to `/home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/tmy_weather.csv` or another valid `.csv` file from the SAM solar_resource directory.
